title: Build skill activation matrix to test trigger behavior
description: Validate that prompting with concrete user inputs activates the right skill. Build a markdown matrix of (prompt, expected skill) pairs covering trigger and anti-trigger cases per skill. Run manually or via scheduled agent before each release. Highest leverage: catches skill-content regressions that frontmatter validation cannot. Source: lead-engineer audit.
close_reason: docs/skill-activation-matrix.md added: 14 skills × 3-4 prompts each (trigger + anti-trigger), plus 5 cross-cutting orchestration checks (using-superpowers ordering, priority rules, completion-claim trigger, override resistance, subagent suppression). Pre-flight + run-log scaffolding included; CONTRIBUTING.md links it as a release gate for SKILL.md edits. The matrix is manual/agent-driven, not part of scripts/preflight.sh, because activation depends on the harness interpreting frontmatter at session start. First exercise of the matrix is a separate post-merge action by a maintainer.

title: Tier A3: Port executing-plans
description: Drop 'load plan file' as the source of truth. Loop on bd ready. Blockers become bd defer or a new blocker issue + bd dep add. Resuming a half-done plan in a new session works without reading the plan file.
close_reason: Closed

title: Tier A2: Port subagent-driven-development
description: Replace TodoWrite-driven dispatch with bd ready / bd update --claim. Subagent reads task brief via bd show <id>. Reviewer subagent calls bd close. State now survives compaction and session end.
close_reason: Closed

title: Tier A1: Port using-superpowers
description: Rewrite using-superpowers/SKILL.md so the flowchart node 'Create TodoWrite todo per item' becomes 'bd create per checklist item'. Update the red-flags table to drop TodoWrite references. This is the highest priority because it's the source of the policy conflict that propagates to every session.
close_reason: Closed

title: EPIC: Beads-native rewrite of superpowers skills
description: Port the 14 superpowers SKILL.md files to use bd as the persistence layer instead of TodoWrite and markdown plan files. See plan in conversation history (Tiers A-D) and README.
close_reason: Completed beads-native rewrite epic; all child ports, formulas, CI, and preflight work closed and final preflight passed.

comments.author: Joseph Bongaarts
comments.text: Verification: all child beads are closed; scripts/preflight.sh -> exit 0 after final skill port; remaining ready list contained only this epic.

title: Automate skill activation matrix runs across claude and codex
description:
Build scripts to drive docs/skill-activation-matrix.md row-by-row in fresh harness sessions, capture which skill (if any) actually activates, and produce a run-log entry.

Scope:
- scripts/run-activation-matrix.sh — single script, --harness=claude|codex. Parses the matrix markdown, runs each row in a fresh non-interactive harness session, writes normalized JSON artifact to .matrix-runs/<ts>-<harness>-<commit>.json.
- scripts/collate-matrix-runs.sh — takes 1+ artifacts (claude local + codex from other machine), produces the markdown run-log block ready to paste into docs/skill-activation-matrix.md.
- Claude path: 'claude -p --output-format stream-json --plugin-dir ./plugins/superpowers-beads --max-turns 2', detect activation via Skill tool_use events.
- Codex path: wire up based on codex docs but mark untested; user validates on a different machine.
- Activation comparator handles 'no skill', 'X or Y', 'X → Y' chains.

Why: the matrix today is a manual 15-20 min per harness check, which is why post-PR-#33 the three deferred rows still got static-reviewed instead of run fresh. Automation closes that gap and makes pre-release matrix runs a one-command operation.
notes: Implementation pushed in PR #35 (branch automate-activation-matrix). Awaiting fresh-session test run + merge. Codex runner is stubbed; user fills in run_codex_row + extract_activations_codex from a codex-capable machine before promoting v0.1.2.

title: Update GH Actions to Node.js 24 before Sept 16 2026 deadline
description: The v0.1.1-rc1 release run emitted: 'Node.js 20 actions are deprecated. The following actions are running on Node.js 20 and may not work as expected: actions/attest-build-provenance/predicate@..., actions/attest@..., actions/checkout@v4. Actions will be forced to run with Node.js 24 by default starting June 2nd, 2026. Node.js 20 will be removed from the runner on September 16th, 2026.' Action: bump to versions that support Node 24 (likely actions/checkout@v5 or pinned to a Node 24 ref; verify actions/attest-build-provenance has a compatible major). Test on a feature branch, then merge before June 2026 to avoid CI breakage. Reference: https://github.blog/changelog/2025-09-19-deprecation-of-node-20-on-github-actions-runners/

title: Add skill cross-reference check to preflight
description: Grep-based check that every superpowers:<name> reference and every ./<file>.{md,sh,...} reference inside SKILL.md files resolves to an actual existing skill or file. Catches silent rot when files are renamed/removed. Wire into scripts/preflight.sh.
close_reason: scripts/check-skill-references.sh added; validates superpowers:<name>, ./<file>, references/<file>, and skills/<plugin>/<file> references; wired into preflight; negative-tested by injecting a broken reference (caught and exit 1).

title: Verify upgrade path delivers new tag content to installed users
description: Smoke-test /plugin marketplace update from a v0.1.0 install in Claude Code (and codex plugin marketplace upgrade in Codex) after we ship a new tag. Document the steps. Establishes that our release flow actually reaches users.

title: RC release dry-run — verify v*.*.*-rc* prerelease flow
description: Tag a v0.1.1-rc1 against a trivial doc change, watch release.yml: confirm tag-version match check passes for prereleases, confirm GH Release is created with prerelease=true (or document that it isn't and decide). Validates the prerelease path before we depend on it for v0.2.
close_reason: release.yml now handles pre-release tags. Trigger pattern adds 'v*.*.*-*'; verify-tag step strips suffix for manifest-version match and exposes prerelease=true/false; gh release create gets --prerelease when applicable. Documented in CHANGELOG and codex-publishing.md. Actual end-to-end exercise (push v0.1.1-rc1) remains a post-merge action by the maintainer; bead is implementation-complete.

title: Add CONTRIBUTING.md for new contributors
description: Document onboarding (clone, bd init --from-jsonl, scripts/preflight.sh), the bd-driven workflow, branch protection / PR flow, skill authoring entry point. Removes the ramp-up cost identified in the audit.
close_reason: CONTRIBUTING.md added with first-time setup, bd workflow, branch/PR flow, skill authoring entry point, validation gates, release process. Replaces ad-hoc onboarding through AGENTS.md alone.

title: Add SECURITY.md vulnerability disclosure path
description: One-page document with contact email and disclosure expectations. Plugin runs shell commands via agents — needs a documented path for reporting issues responsibly.
close_reason: SECURITY.md added with disclosure email, threat model (in-scope: skill-instruction prompt-injection vectors, supplementary file rule bypass, scripts with input-validation gaps, release pipeline; out-of-scope: harness prompt injection, bd internals, upstream obra/superpowers), coordinated disclosure terms.

title: Wrap up multi-agent swarm integration
description: Integrate the branches and PRs from the multi-agent swarm. Confirm this agent's pushed branches are merged, review open PRs from the other agent, merge clean PRs, and update beads as needed.
acceptance_criteria: All clean swarm branches/PRs are merged to main; any blocked or rejected PR has a bead note explaining why; associated beads reflect merged or blocked state; final status identifies remaining work.
close_reason: Merged this agent's completed branches, merged clean PRs #18 and #23, integrated reviewed PRs #20/#21/#22 locally for main push, and documented #17 as stale/regressive.

comments.author: Joseph Bongaarts
comments.text: Integration decisions: #23 merged this agent's Codex startup/publishing/instruction branches; #18 reviewed, refreshed, and merged; #20/#21/#22 reviewed clean but required conflict resolution against current main and are integrated locally for direct push; #17 is not clean after #23 and should remain unmerged because it would regress newer Codex tool-reference guidance while duplicating a fix already on main.

title: Judgement: design the dual Claude/Codex publish pipeline (fxd publish half)
description:
superpowers-beads-fxd has two halves: PR/main CI gates (workable; being split into smaller beads) and a release/publish pipeline (NOT workable autonomously — needs human design decisions).

Open questions the publish pipeline needs answered before implementation:
- Where do Codex artifacts get published? (GH release asset, marketplace catalog, separate repo?)
- Do we sign or attest artifacts? If yes, with what mechanism (sigstore, GH attestations, GPG)?
- How are publish secrets scoped — per-repo Actions secrets, environment-protected, or org-level?
- Should a failed Codex publish roll back the Claude publish, or is best-effort acceptable?
- Which event triggers a release — git tag, GH release, or both?
- Release-notes/changelog generation: hand-rolled, conventional commits, or a tool?

Until these are answered, an autonomous agent cannot build the publish pipeline without picking one option per question and pretending it's the right one. Filing this judgement bead so the human can decide and the publish-half of fxd can become workable.
notes: Filed during autonomous session as a judgement-call follow-up. Deferred until human review per the user's session-start instructions.
close_reason: Defaults accepted: tag-triggered (v*.*.*) GH Actions workflow, source tarball + sha256 attached to GH Release with auto-generated notes, GitHub-native build-provenance attestation (no GPG/sigstore), no extra registry, no rollback (single commit is the install source for both harnesses). Implementation in .github/workflows/release.yml.

title: Validate Codex manifest and marketplace in CI
description:
Extend CI/release checks after Codex packaging exists so publishing regressions are caught before release.

Scope:
- Validate plugins/superpowers-beads/.codex-plugin/plugin.json schema/required fields.
- Validate .agents/plugins/marketplace.json entries, paths, and policies.
- Check version consistency across Claude and Codex manifests/catalogs.
- Fold these checks into the broader CI/publish workflow.
acceptance_criteria: CI fails on invalid Codex plugin manifest or marketplace JSON; version drift across Claude/Codex manifests is detected; checks are integrated into the full CI/publish workflow.
close_reason: Implemented scripts/check-codex-manifests.sh validating required fields (name/version/description/skills/interface.displayName) and source-path resolution for both Codex plugin manifest and marketplace; extended check-version-sync.sh to include Codex plugin.json so version drift between Claude and Codex manifests now fails preflight; both wired into scripts/preflight.sh so CI catches regressions.

title: Add Codex plugin manifest and marketplace catalog
description:
Implement the Codex publishing plan from docs/codex-publishing.md by adding a Codex plugin manifest and repo marketplace catalog.

Scope:
- Add plugins/superpowers-beads/.codex-plugin/plugin.json with complete install-surface metadata.
- Add .agents/plugins/marketplace.json pointing at plugins/superpowers-beads.
- Keep paths relative and compatible with Codex marketplace loading.
- Do not claim the skills are fully Codex-compatible until superpowers-beads-zr6 has landed.
acceptance_criteria: The repo contains a valid .codex-plugin/plugin.json for superpowers-beads; .agents/plugins/marketplace.json exposes the plugin for Codex; install docs can point at codex plugin marketplace add; the implementation remains blocked until the agnostic skill work is complete.
close_reason: Implemented Codex plugin manifest, repository marketplace catalog, install docs, and preflight JSON checks.

comments.author: Joseph Bongaarts
comments.text: Implemented Codex plugin metadata: added plugins/superpowers-beads/.codex-plugin/plugin.json, .agents/plugins/marketplace.json, Codex install docs, and JSON parse checks in scripts/preflight.sh. Verification: jq empty on both Codex JSON files, git diff --check, and env ALLOW_IN_PROGRESS=superpowers-beads-dg3 scripts/preflight.sh passed.

title: Design Codex plugin publishing and installation path
description:
Existing agnostic-plugin work covers making the skills themselves Codex-friendly. This issue is specifically for deciding how Codex users discover, install, update, and trust the plugin once those skills are compatible.

Research Codex's current plugin and skill installation mechanisms, then propose a publishing model. Compare at least: GitHub release assets, a marketplace-style index, direct git/repo install, and generated artifacts from shared source. Include how versions, checksums or provenance, docs, and upgrade instructions work for Codex consumers.

The plan should account for both maintainers publishing official artifacts and contributors or users installing from a fork or local checkout.
acceptance_criteria: A concrete Codex publishing/install plan is documented; the plan is distinct from skill content compatibility work; it covers discovery, install, update, versioning, and artifact trust; it supports official releases plus fork/local checkout use; follow-up implementation beads are filed if the chosen plan requires build or release automation.
close_reason: Codex publishing/install plan documented; follow-up implementation and decision-review beads filed.

comments.author: Joseph Bongaarts
comments.text: Documented Codex publishing/install plan in docs/codex-publishing.md. Chosen path: repo-hosted Codex marketplace with .agents/plugins/marketplace.json plus plugins/superpowers-beads/.codex-plugin/plugin.json; release tags for stable installs; direct skill install only as local/dev fallback. Follow-ups filed: superpowers-beads-dg3, superpowers-beads-4pp; judgement review deferred: superpowers-beads-soj.

title: Handle sessions when beads is not installed
description:
When a session starts and the beads CLI is not installed, the plugin should fail gracefully and make the next step explicit without assuming beads is mandatory for this repo or that the user can install tools globally.

The flow should detect missing bd separately from repo initialization problems. It should support different user roles and permission levels: a maintainer who wants to adopt beads, a contributor in someone else's repo, or a user who simply wants to use the skills without beads for this session.

Likely outcomes include: continue without beads for the session, show install options appropriate to the platform, use a repo-local or temporary install path where feasible, or disable beads-specific workflows with clear messaging.
acceptance_criteria: Missing bd is detected before any beads command is required; the user can continue without beads for that session; install guidance avoids assuming global install permissions or maintainer status; repo initialization and CLI installation are handled as separate states; behavior is documented and covered by tests or scripted checks.
close_reason: Missing-bd startup path documented and wired into entry/worktree skills; validation passed.

comments.author: Joseph Bongaarts
comments.text: Implemented missing-bd startup handling: docs/beads-startup.md documents non-fatal CLI-missing behavior; using-superpowers checks command -v bd before requiring beads commands; using-git-worktrees handles missing bd before bd worktree commands; README clarifies beads-backed requirements. Verification: claude plugin validate . passed; ALLOW_IN_PROGRESS=superpowers-beads-422 scripts/preflight.sh passed.

title: Handle sessions in repos without beads initialized
description:
When the plugin starts in a repository that does not have beads initialized, it should handle that state intentionally instead of assuming beads is available or that the user wants to initialize it.

The flow should distinguish between: no beads metadata in this repo, beads metadata exists but is invalid, and user is outside a repo. It should explain options without assuming the user is a maintainer with permission to add project files, and without assuming a contributor wants to introduce beads to that repo.

Likely outcomes include a neutral prompt or documented decision path for: continue without beads for this session, initialize beads locally or in the repo when appropriate, or point at existing project tracking if beads is not desired.
acceptance_criteria: Starting a session in a repo with no beads metadata produces a clear non-fatal path; users can continue without beads for that session; initialization is never automatic without explicit user intent; messaging does not assume maintainer privileges; contributor-safe behavior is documented and covered by tests or scripted checks.
close_reason: No-active-beads-workspace startup path documented and wired into entry/worktree skills; validation passed.

comments.author: Joseph Bongaarts
comments.text: Implemented no-active-workspace startup handling: docs/beads-startup.md now documents bd where --json, no_beads_directory, outside-repo detection, and degraded metadata behavior; using-superpowers and using-git-worktrees distinguish missing CLI from missing workspace and avoid automatic bd init. Verification: bd where --json from /tmp produced expected no_beads_directory; claude plugin validate . passed; ALLOW_IN_PROGRESS=superpowers-beads-2ko scripts/preflight.sh passed.

title: Consolidate AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md to a single source of truth
description:
AGENTS.md (read by Codex and other agnostic harnesses) and CLAUDE.md (read by Claude) duplicate content and have drifted apart:
- AGENTS.md has a non-interactive shell rules section that CLAUDE.md does not.
- CLAUDE.md has the repo-layout / conventions / preflight section that AGENTS.md does not.
- Both contain a beads-integration block, but only AGENTS.md's is auto-managed.

Result: Codex sees layout-blind instructions, Claude sees shell-rule-blind instructions. Inconsistent guidance defeats both files.

Pick one source of truth (probably AGENTS.md, since superpowers-beads-zr6 wants the plugin agnostic). Make the other file a thin pointer or symlink. Keep the auto-managed beads block intact in whichever file beads writes to.
acceptance_criteria: Layout, conventions, preflight, beads rules, and shell-safety rules all appear once and are reachable from both Claude and Codex sessions.
notes: Tightly coupled to superpowers-beads-zr6 (Make plugin Claude/Codex agnostic). Likely should be done as part of that effort, but file separately so it is not lost.
close_reason: Closed

title: Run scripts/preflight.sh in CI
description:
.github/workflows/validate.yml only runs 'claude plugin validate .'. The canonical gate per docs/preflight.md and CLAUDE.md is scripts/preflight.sh, which additionally runs git diff --check, bd orphans, bd stale, and the in-progress check. Today PRs can land with stale/orphaned beads and unresolved in-progress issues.

This is a subset of the broader CI work in superpowers-beads-fxd, but is small and unblocked: just install bd in CI and run preflight.sh. Do this before fxd to stop further drift.
acceptance_criteria: CI installs bd, runs scripts/preflight.sh on PR + main, and fails the workflow on a non-zero exit.
notes:
Implementation complete on local branch ci/preflight-13i (commit af1683e in /tmp/sb-13i): updated .github/workflows/validate.yml to install bd from gastownhall/beads v1.0.3 release with sha256 checksum verification and run scripts/preflight.sh. Verified locally — preflight passes when no other beads are in_progress.

BLOCKER: cannot push branch — the gh OAuth token in this environment has scopes admin:public_key, gist, read:org, repo but lacks 'workflow' scope, so HTTPS push of files under .github/workflows/ is rejected. The SSH key (~/.ssh/id_rsa) requires a passphrase prompt that ssh-askpass cannot answer in this non-interactive sandbox.

Unblock options: run 'gh auth refresh -h github.com -s workflow' interactively, or load the SSH key into ssh-agent before the next autonomous session.

Once unblocked: cd /tmp/sb-13i && git push -u origin ci/preflight-13i, then 'gh pr create --title "Run scripts/preflight.sh in CI" --body "Closes superpowers-beads-13i."'.
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: Joseph Bongaarts
comments.text: Autonomous session set-aside: implementation already exists in /tmp/sb-13i at af1683e, but pushing workflow-file changes is blocked by GitHub auth lacking workflow scope. Needs interactive gh auth refresh -h github.com -s workflow or SSH key loaded into ssh-agent, then push ci/preflight-13i.

title: Reconcile Task tool (superpowers:code-reviewer) usage with no registered code-reviewer agent
description:
plugins/superpowers-beads/skills/subagent-driven-development/code-quality-reviewer-prompt.md and SKILL.md (and writing-plans/plan-issue-reviewer-prompt.md, brainstorming/epic-reviewer-prompt.md, the other subagent-driven-development reviewer prompts) all instruct the controller to dispatch a named agent like 'superpowers:code-reviewer'. plugin.json does not register any agents — there is no 'agents' field and no agents/ directory. On Claude the named-agent dispatch will fail; on Codex it cannot work at all (see references/codex-tools.md).

Either:
- Register an actual code-reviewer agent in the plugin (add agents/ directory + manifest entry once Claude/Codex schemas are known), or
- Rewrite the prompts to dispatch a generic agent with the inline prompt content from code-reviewer.md.

This overlaps with the agnostic-plugin work (superpowers-beads-zr6), but the fix is needed regardless of harness because the current state is broken on Claude too.
acceptance_criteria: Either an agents/ entry exists for code-reviewer (and any other named agents the prompts reference), or the prompts dispatch generic agents with inline content. No skill instructs the controller to call a named agent that the plugin does not register.
close_reason: Closed

title: Fix broken cross-skill template reference in subagent-driven-development
description: plugins/superpowers-beads/skills/subagent-driven-development/code-quality-reviewer-prompt.md:11 instructs the controller to 'Use template at requesting-code-review/code-reviewer.md', but the relative path is wrong from this skill's directory — the file lives at ../requesting-code-review/code-reviewer.md. The reference will fail wherever an agent resolves it as written. Fix the path, or change to a stable cross-skill reference form (e.g., superpowers:requesting-code-review/code-reviewer.md) once an agnostic format is decided.
acceptance_criteria: The reference resolves from the skill's directory, OR the skill no longer points at another skill's file.
close_reason: Fixed in small-fixes-bundle branch; claude plugin validate passes.

title: Thorough code review of current repo state
description:
Do a thorough code review of the entire superpowers-beads repo as it stands today. Goal: surface bugs, inconsistencies, drift from conventions, security issues, and opportunities to simplify before the harness-agnostic restructure (zr6) lands.

Scope:
- Marketplace + plugin manifests (.claude-plugin/marketplace.json, plugins/superpowers-beads/.claude-plugin/plugin.json) — schema correctness, version sync, source paths.
- Every SKILL.md — frontmatter completeness, internal consistency, dead links, attribution to obra/superpowers where applicable, accuracy of any commands/snippets they instruct the agent to run.
- Workflow formulas (any superpowers workflow / formula files).
- scripts/preflight.sh and docs/preflight.md — does the script match what the doc claims, does it actually catch what it should.
- Top-level docs (README.md, CLAUDE.md) — accuracy vs. current code.
- Repo hygiene: stray files (.codex was untracked at session start), gitignore correctness, license headers.

Output: a structured review report (findings grouped by severity) filed back into beads as follow-up issues for anything actionable.
acceptance_criteria:
- Every file in the repo has been read at least once during the review.
- Findings are categorized (bug / inconsistency / convention drift / nit) with file:line references.
- Each actionable finding becomes its own bead, linked back to this review issue.
- A short summary is posted in this issue's notes.
notes:
Review complete. 10 follow-up beads filed:

Bug:
- superpowers-beads-dsl (P2) — Fix broken cross-skill template reference in subagent-driven-development
- superpowers-beads-tnv (P2) — Reconcile Task tool (superpowers:code-reviewer) usage with no registered code-reviewer agent
- superpowers-beads-7o7 (P3) — Harden in-progress detection in scripts/preflight.sh

Task (CI/process):
- superpowers-beads-13i (P2) — Run scripts/preflight.sh in CI [now blocks fxd]
- superpowers-beads-bl0 (P2) — Consolidate AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md [blocked by zr6]

Task (docs/cleanup):
- superpowers-beads-95m (P3) — Refresh README Status section
- superpowers-beads-ndy (P3) — Fix brainstorming description and Gemini auto-load claim
- superpowers-beads-s1v (P4) — Replace stale issue ID example in docs/preflight.md
- superpowers-beads-nc5 (P4) — Decide on .codex root file
- superpowers-beads-0rd (P4) — Parameterize systematic-debugging/find-polluter.sh

No code changes from this review pass — review-only scope.

Verification: `claude plugin validate .` -> exit 0 (validation passed) confirms manifest health at the time of review.
close_reason: Thorough code review complete; 10 follow-up beads filed with file:line references. Plugin validate passed at time of review.

title: Build full CI workflow with dual Claude/Codex publishing
description:
The repo has scripts/preflight.sh but no CI. We need a proper CI workflow that runs on every PR and on main, plus a publish pipeline that releases the plugin for both Claude Code and Codex consumers.

Scope:
- CI (PRs + main):
  - Run scripts/preflight.sh (and bd preflight where appropriate).
  - Validate marketplace.json / plugin.json schemas.
  - Validate skill frontmatter (name, description present; lint conventions).
  - Verify version bump consistency between marketplace.json and plugin.json.
  - Run any unit/integration checks we add.
- Release/publish:
  - Trigger on tag or release.
  - Produce + publish a Claude marketplace artifact (current shape).
  - Produce + publish a Codex-compatible artifact (shape TBD by the agnostic-plugin work).
  - Generate release notes / changelog.
- Caching, matrix (if needed for multiple harness validators), and concurrency guards.

Out of scope: the actual harness-agnostic restructure (separate bead). This issue assumes that work has produced a build target per harness and wires CI around it.
design:
Likely GitHub Actions. Decisions to make:
- Where do Codex artifacts get published (registry, GH release asset, separate repo)? Mirrors the Claude marketplace question.
- Do we sign/verify artifacts?
- How are secrets scoped for the publish job?
acceptance_criteria:
- PRs are gated on a green CI run that exercises preflight + schema/frontmatter validation + version-bump check.
- main is protected and runs the same gates.
- A tagged release produces and publishes artifacts for both Claude and Codex consumers.
- Failed publishes do not leave half-released state (atomic or clearly recoverable).
notes:
Scope split during autonomous session:
- CI gates (preflight + schema + version-sync + frontmatter lint): superpowers-beads-13i (preflight in CI, PR #21), superpowers-beads-zo1 (frontmatter lint), superpowers-beads-6z6 (version-sync). All three are concrete and shipping.
- Publish pipeline (Codex artifact target, signing, secrets, release trigger): superpowers-beads-f3i — judgement bead; needs human design decisions before implementation.

This umbrella bead now depends on zo1, 6z6, and f3i. Once those resolve, the umbrella can close.
close_reason: Umbrella complete: all gates (preflight + version-sync incl. Codex + frontmatter lint + Codex manifest validation) run in CI on PRs/main. Publish pipeline implemented in .github/workflows/release.yml: tag v*.*.* -> preflight, source tarball, sha256, GH Actions build-provenance attestation, GH Release with auto-generated notes.

title: Make plugin Claude/Codex agnostic
description:
The plugin is currently structured solely for Claude Code (.claude-plugin/, plugin.json, marketplace.json, SKILL.md frontmatter conventions). We want it to be usable from both Claude Code and Codex so the same skill content can be consumed by either harness.

Scope:
- Audit current Claude-specific assumptions (directory layout, manifest schemas, frontmatter fields, skill invocation conventions, hooks/settings references).
- Research Codex's equivalent plugin/skill packaging format and identify the delta vs. Claude.
- Decide on a structure: shared source-of-truth for skill bodies + harness-specific manifests, or a build step that emits per-harness artifacts, or dual-format files where viable.
- Update README/CLAUDE.md to describe the dual-target model.
- Migrate existing skills (test-driven-development, using-git-worktroots, writing-skills, dispatching-parallel-agents, superpowers workflow formulas) to the new structure without regressing Claude behavior.

Out of scope: the actual CI/publish pipeline (tracked separately).
design:
Open questions to resolve during design:
- Is there a single manifest format both harnesses accept, or do we need per-harness manifests generated from a shared source?
- Where do Claude-specific concepts (hooks, slash commands, settings.json) map in Codex, and what happens to skills that rely on them?
- Should the repo rename from 'superpowers-beads' (Claude-marketplace flavored) to a harness-neutral name, or keep the name and just broaden scope?
acceptance_criteria:
- Skills load and run correctly under both Claude Code and Codex from a single checkout.
- No skill is silently Claude-only without an explicit, documented opt-out.
- README documents how to install/use the plugin from each harness.
- Existing Claude users see no regression.
close_reason: Shared skills are discoverable by Claude and Codex from one checkout; docs and preflight updated; validation passed.

comments.author: Joseph Bongaarts
comments.text: Implemented checkout-level Codex compatibility: added .agents/skills symlink to shared skill source, documented Claude/Codex consumption in README and CLAUDE.md, added Codex discovery smoke check to scripts/preflight.sh, updated using-superpowers for Codex skill activation, replaced unsupported named-agent dispatch in code-quality reviewer prompt with generic inline dispatch, and refreshed Codex/Copilot tool mappings. Verification: find -L .agents/skills found all 14 SKILL.md files; claude plugin validate . passed; ALLOW_IN_PROGRESS=superpowers-beads-zr6 scripts/preflight.sh passed.

title: Patch pre-commit hook for missing timeout binary
description: Beads' .beads/hooks/pre-commit looks for the 'timeout' binary but macOS doesn't ship it (coreutils provides 'gtimeout'). When timeout is missing, the hook silently runs without a timeout and can deadlock. Patch the hook to also check for gtimeout, or fall through to a perl-alarm shim. This bit us during bd init in this repo. Consider upstreaming to beads as well.
close_reason: Patched pre-commit hook to use timeout, gtimeout, then a Perl alarm shim before falling back without timeout.

title: Create GitHub remote and decide visibility

title: Author beads formulas for superpowers workflows

title: Tier B5: Port requesting-code-review

title: Tier B4: Port receiving-code-review
description: Each unresolved feedback item becomes a child task linked to the parent. Evaluations go on as comments (bd comment), not in chat history.
close_reason: Ported receiving-code-review so feedback evaluations are stored as bd comments and accepted unresolved items become child tasks on the parent issue.

title: Tier B3: Port systematic-debugging
description: Open a --type=bug issue at Phase 1; append evidence with bd note; at root-cause, bd remember --key=<topic> so the lesson is reusable across sessions.
close_reason: Ported systematic-debugging to track debugging sessions as bd bug issues, append evidence and hypotheses as notes, and persist reusable root-cause lessons with bd remember.

title: Tier B2: Port brainstorming
description: Final-approval step creates the feature epic in bd with --design and --acceptance populated. Markdown spec becomes optional export.
close_reason: Ported brainstorming to create beads-native feature epics with design and acceptance fields, optional exports, and copied visual companion support files.

title: Tier B1: Port writing-plans
description: Output is an epic + child tasks rather than (or in addition to) a markdown file. Keep markdown as a generated export via bd export / templated bd show so reviewers still get a human-readable artifact.
close_reason: Ported writing-plans as a beads-native epic and child issue planning workflow with optional export guidance.

title: Scaffold superpowers-beads repo
description: Initial scaffold (commit 36724a2): marketplace.json, plugin.json, README, MIT LICENSE preserving upstream attribution to obra/superpowers, CLAUDE.md, empty plugins/superpowers-beads/skills/ tree, git init. Migrated from ~/.beads (was jhbongaarts-dg5).
close_reason: Scaffold complete in commit 36724a2

title: Tighten using-superpowers override-acknowledgment behavior or relax matrix row 4
description: Cross-cutting matrix row 4 expects: when user says 'skip the skill, just do it', the agent announces the relevant skill and only opts out with explicit recorded override. The using-superpowers Red Flags table is about self-rationalization, not user-explicit override; the Instruction Priority section says user wins. So either (a) add one line to using-superpowers prescribing 'announce + acknowledge override' before skipping a skill on user instruction, or (b) relax matrix row 4 to accept silent compliance with user-explicit override. Either resolves the gap surfaced by the v0.1.1-rc1 matrix run.

title: Exercise visual-companion + find-polluter in CI or document them as deferred
description: brainstorming/scripts/server.cjs (visual companion) and systematic-debugging/find-polluter.sh have never been run in this repo. Either add a smoke test (server starts, find-polluter handles a known-bad test fixture) or mark them experimental in their parent skills.

title: Add bd API compatibility tests for commands referenced in skills
description: Skills reference bd lint, bd mol pour, specific formula step names, bd ready --explain, bd preflight, etc. If bd renames or restructures any of these, skills break silently. Add a small smoke test (perhaps in CI) that exercises each bd command we depend on with the current pinned bd version.

title: Establish process for tracking obra/superpowers upstream
description: We are a rewrite, not a fork. Upstream evolves (5.x). Define a periodic diff/port cadence (e.g., quarterly review of obra/superpowers HEAD vs our last sync point) and where to record decisions about which upstream improvements to port.

title: Test cross-platform compatibility (Windows native, Codex, Copilot, Gemini)
description: Skills claim multi-harness support but CI runs Linux only and the maintainer's daily driver is macOS. Run a baseline activation check on each claimed harness; document any gaps. Focus on shell assumptions in supplementary scripts.

title: Establish user feedback channel + light usage signal
description: Currently no support venue (Issues? Discussions? README contact?) and no install/activation telemetry. Decide whether to enable GitHub Discussions, link a contact, or add an opt-in feedback path. Otherwise we ship blind.

title: Define deprecation/support policy for skills and bd versions
description: If we rename a skill or change its trigger, what notice do users get? Which bd versions do we support and for how long? Encode in CHANGELOG / RELEASING. Currently informal.

title: Pin/dependabot CI dependencies (claude-code npm, bd)
description: CI runs npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code (latest, unpinned). bd is pinned to BD_VERSION. Add a version constraint or dependabot config so silent upstream regressions are caught before they break our CI/release.

title: Document rollback / bad-release remediation
description: Tags can't be cleanly unpublished. Document the remediation path: yank GH Release, ship superseding tag, communicate via release notes + CHANGELOG. Decide whether to delete the bad tag or retain with note.

title: Document release cadence and versioning policy
description: Write a short RELEASING.md (or section in CONTRIBUTING.md): cadence (ad-hoc vs scheduled), what counts as a major/minor/patch bump for a skills plugin, when v1.0.0 ships. Currently undocumented.

title: Document hotfix protocol for critical bugs in a tagged release
description: If v0.1.0 ships with a critical bug, what is the procedure to ship v0.1.1 quickly without bundling other in-flight changes? Branch from tag, cherry-pick fix, tag, release. Document the steps.

title: Audit cleanup: parallel-burst formula, doc staleness, category normalization, CHANGELOG, hooks doc
description: Wire superpowers-parallel-burst into dispatching-parallel-agents. Clean up done/stale items in docs/codex-publishing.md. Soften 'canonical' bd preflight wording in finishing-a-development-branch SKILL.md. Normalize marketplace category to Productivity across all three manifests and extend codex-manifests check. Add CHANGELOG.md. Document bd hooks install for new contributors in AGENTS.md.
close_reason: Audit cleanup landed: parallel-burst formula wired into dispatching-parallel-agents (Step 1 prototype-burst path); docs/codex-publishing.md cleaned of done items and stale bead reference; finishing-a-development-branch SKILL.md prefers project preflight script when present; marketplace category normalized to 'Productivity' across all three manifests with cross-manifest check in scripts/check-codex-manifests.sh; CHANGELOG.md added with v0.1.0 + Unreleased entries; AGENTS.md gained First-time setup section explaining bd init --from-jsonl for hooks. Preflight passes.

title: Wire bd lint and formulas into skills (tiers 1-3)
description: Replace model-driven self-review and manual bead chains with deterministic tools. Tier 1: bd lint in brainstorming/writing-plans self-review; superpowers-skill-authoring formula in writing-skills. Tier 2: superpowers-bugfix and superpowers-code-review-response formulas. Tier 3: superpowers-feature formula optional pour from brainstorming.
close_reason: Replaced model-driven self-review/manual chain creation with deterministic bd tools across 6 skills. Tier 1: writing-skills now pours superpowers-skill-authoring; brainstorming and writing-plans self-review now lead with bd lint and only do judgment checks afterwards. Tier 2: systematic-debugging and receiving-code-review reference superpowers-bugfix and superpowers-code-review-response formulas as the default for non-trivial work. Tier 3: brainstorming surfaces superpowers-feature as an optional implementation-chain pour after epic approval; writing-plans and executing-plans note how to claim formula steps when the chain has been poured.

close_reason: Trimmed plugin token surface by ~300 lines (~10%) without changing prescribed behavior. using-superpowers down 184->125 lines (always-loaded). Replaced DOT graphs with numbered lists in using-superpowers, brainstorming, subagent-driven-development. Compressed bd-availability prose in using-superpowers and deduped Step 0 of using-git-worktrees. Compressed verbose example in subagent-driven-development. Tightened verbose frontmatter descriptions on verification-before-completion, using-git-worktrees, receiving-code-review. Removed stale 'when ported' parenthetical in executing-plans. Normalized attribution comments. Deleted two orphaned reviewer-prompt templates (epic-reviewer-prompt.md, plan-issue-reviewer-prompt.md) that were never referenced.

Subset of superpowers-beads-fxd scope: 'Verify version bump consistency between marketplace.json and plugin.json'. Filed separately so it can ship without waiting on the publish-pipeline design decisions in fxd.

title: Fix brainstorming SKILL description and Gemini auto-load claim

Two small inconsistencies in skill metadata/description:

1. plugins/superpowers-beads/skills/brainstorming/SKILL.md frontmatter description starts with 'You MUST use this...'. Every other skill (and the writing-skills convention itself, SKILL.md:96) starts with 'Use when...'. Bring brainstorming in line.

2. plugins/superpowers-beads/skills/using-superpowers/SKILL.md:41 claims 'Gemini CLI users get the tool mapping loaded automatically via GEMINI.md'. There is no GEMINI.md shipped by this plugin, so the auto-load claim is misleading. Either ship one or rephrase to say users must reference references/gemini-tools.md themselves.

The tier system is not referenced in any skill or doc. Either update the status to reflect current state (skills ported, listing them) or drop the section in favor of the bead tracker as source of truth.
acceptance_criteria: README accurately reflects what is in main today.
notes: Trivial; bundle with the next docs PR.
close_reason: Fixed in small-fixes-bundle branch; claude plugin validate passes.

title: Harden in-progress detection in scripts/preflight.sh
description:
scripts/preflight.sh has two fragility issues in its in-progress check:

1. Line 18: 'in_progress_count=$(... | grep -c "\"id\":")' counts JSON 'id' keys with a regex grep. This will overcount if an id-shaped string appears inside another field's value, and undercount on minified JSON.
2. Line 24: 'grep -q "\"id\": \"$allowed_in_progress\""' interprets the variable as a regex. The example documented in docs/preflight.md (superpowers-beads-isj.1) contains a literal '.' that grep treats as 'any character'.

Fix by using jq (preferred) or grep -F for fixed-string matching, and by counting via 'jq length' instead of grep -c.
acceptance_criteria: Preflight correctly counts in-progress issues from arbitrary bd JSON output, and the ALLOW_IN_PROGRESS match is exact-string.
close_reason: Replaced grep-based JSON count with 'jq length' and switched the allow-list match to a fixed-string jq existence check. Verified: ALLOW_IN_PROGRESS=superpowers-beads-7o7 ./scripts/preflight.sh -> 'Plugin preflight passed.'

title: Decision review: Codex repo skills use .agents symlink
description:
Judgement call made while resolving superpowers-beads-zr6: Codex checkout compatibility is implemented by adding .agents/skills as a symlink to plugins/superpowers-beads/skills, rather than copying skill directories or introducing a generated build output for repo-local use.

Reasoning: this keeps one skill source of truth, matches Codex repo-skill discovery, avoids drift between Claude and Codex copies, and leaves installable Codex plugin packaging to superpowers-beads-dg3.

This bead is intentionally for human review after the autonomous session. Keep the decision if symlinks are acceptable in this repo; revise it if cross-platform constraints require generated copies or a build step instead.
acceptance_criteria: Human reviews the .agents/skills symlink decision and either closes this bead as accepted or files/updates implementation work to use generated/copy-based Codex skill discovery instead.
close_reason: Accepted: keep .agents/skills as a symlink to plugins/superpowers-beads/skills. Sole Windows contributor works via WSL2, where symlinks resolve correctly. Plugin consumers install via marketplace and use plugins/superpowers-beads/skills/ directly per the Codex manifest's skills field, so they're unaffected. Revisit if a non-WSL Windows contributor appears: the migration path is to commit .agents/skills as a real directory and add a diff -r preflight check.

title: Judgement: confirm .codex file disposition (gitignore vs delete)
description:
While closing superpowers-beads-nc5 I chose to .gitignore the 0-byte .codex root file rather than delete it. Reason: a local Codex agent was running concurrently and might be using .codex as a marker; gitignoring is reversible and risk-free, deletion is not.

If on inspection the file is truly orphaned, please:
- rm .codex
- (Optional) keep the .gitignore entry so future local Codex sessions don't reintroduce it.

If Codex DOES need the file (e.g. as a feature flag, harness marker, or auto-created stub), please document why and either track it or leave the .gitignore entry in place.

This bead exists only to let a human revisit the call once they're back at the keyboard.
notes: Filed as a judgement-call follow-up per the user's session-start instructions. Not workable autonomously — needs human review of local Codex behavior.
close_reason: Accepted: keep .codex in .gitignore. The marker was created by a local Codex session on a different machine; no consumer of this repo needs it tracked, and gitignoring stays cheap and reversible.

title: Decision review: Codex marketplace stays in this repo
description:
Judgement call made while resolving superpowers-beads-x3i: the documented plan keeps Codex distribution in this repository by adding .agents/plugins/marketplace.json and plugins/superpowers-beads/.codex-plugin/plugin.json, rather than creating a separate registry repo or publishing only GitHub release assets.

Reasoning: Codex supports repo marketplaces, this keeps release versions with the skills and manifests, and fork/local checkout installs remain simple. Release assets/checksums can be added later without making them the primary install surface.

This bead is intentionally for human review after the autonomous session. Keep the decision if this repo should be the source of truth for Codex installation; revise it if you prefer a separate marketplace repo, an official curated submission first, or release-asset-only installs.
acceptance_criteria: Human reviews the publishing-channel decision and either closes this bead as accepted or files/updates implementation work to use a different Codex distribution channel.
close_reason: Accepted: keep Codex marketplace in this repo via .agents/plugins/marketplace.json + plugins/superpowers-beads/.codex-plugin/plugin.json. Right shape for v0.1 with one plugin; release.yml ships tag = install source for both Claude and Codex consumers. Cheap to layer on a separate marketplace repo or curated submission later if needed.

title: Parameterize systematic-debugging/find-polluter.sh test runner
description:
plugins/superpowers-beads/skills/systematic-debugging/find-polluter.sh:42 hardcodes 'npm test "$TEST_FILE"'. The script ships in a general-purpose plugin and may run in non-Node projects. Either:
- Add a TEST_CMD environment variable defaulting to 'npm test' so users in pytest/go/cargo projects can override.
- Document that the script is Node-specific in its header.
acceptance_criteria: Either the script is parameterized via env var, or the limitation is documented in the script header and in the SKILL that references it.
close_reason: Closed

title: Decide on .codex root file (remove or document)
description:
A 0-byte read-only file '.codex' exists at the repo root. It is untracked, not in .gitignore, and not referenced in any doc, manifest, or script. Likely an artifact from local Codex experimentation. Two options:

1. Delete it and add '.codex' to .gitignore if Codex creates such markers locally.
2. If it is intentionally a marker for the agnostic-plugin work (zr6), document why and either track it or .gitignore it.

Decide and act so 'git status' is clean.
acceptance_criteria: The repo root has no untracked '.codex' surprise — either deleted, ignored, or tracked-and-explained.
close_reason: Chose to .gitignore .codex rather than delete the 0-byte file, since a local Codex session is actively running in this repo and may be using it as a marker. The .gitignore entry satisfies the acceptance criteria (no untracked .codex surprise) without disturbing the parallel Codex agent's working state. If the file turns out to be a leftover with no live use, deleting it is a one-line follow-up.

title: Replace stale issue ID example in docs/preflight.md
description: docs/preflight.md:20 references 'superpowers-beads-isj.1' as the ALLOW_IN_PROGRESS example. That issue and its sub-issue are closed; the literal id no longer means anything to a reader. Replace with a generic placeholder like '<bead-implementing-preflight>' or '$ACTIVE_BEAD'.
acceptance_criteria: No stale concrete issue IDs remain in docs/preflight.md.
close_reason: Fixed in small-fixes-bundle branch; claude plugin validate passes.

value: Pushing branches that touch .github/workflows/ files from this sandbox fails: the gh OAuth token in this env has scopes admin:public_key, gist, read:org, repo but lacks 'workflow' scope. SSH falls back to id_rsa which needs a passphrase that ssh-askpass cannot answer. Unblock with 'gh auth refresh -h github.com -s workflow' (interactive) or by loading id_rsa into ssh-agent before launching the autonomous session.
